video:video.video:title: Sirens sound to warn of an incoming missile in central Israel
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded to warn of an incoming missile in Central Israel on Wednesday. An AP reporter heard loud booms in Tel Aviv as the windows of buildings shook from reverberations.

video:video.video:description: Airstrikes continued to hit Tehran followed by plumes of smoke rising above buildings in Iran's capital.

video:video.video:description: Four Artemis II mission astronauts launched into space from Florida's Kennedy Space Center for humanity’s first trip to the moon in more than half a century.

video:video.video:description: Marine archaeologists are racing to unearth the Danish warship Dannebroge, which took heavy fire from British Admiral Horatio Nelson’s fleet and later exploded during the 1801 Battle of Copenhagen.

video:video.video:description: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors

video:video.video:description: After concluding decades-long careers at hospitals, universities and corporations, retirees are returning to the workforce due to insufficient retirement savings, rising living costs and a desire to stay active.

video:video.video:description: The largest monthly jump in gas prices in six decades caused a sharp spike in inflation in March. It’s the first read on inflation to capture the effects of the Iran war.

video:video.video:description: Check out some of Artemis II's best moments from NASA's historic lunar comeback!

video:video.video:title: Artemis II returns home with Pacific splashdown
video:video.video:description: Artemis II’s astronauts returned from the moon with a dramatic splashdown in the Pacific on Friday to close out humanity’s first lunar voyage in more than a half-century.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and the U.S. military say a U.S. blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas will begin on Monday.

video:video.video:description: Raja Ampat, a remote archipelago in eastern Indonesia, has been long regarded as a model for ocean conservation.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he won’t apologize to Pope Leo
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he would not apologize for sharply criticizing Pope Leo saying that the pontiff “went public” in his criticisms of the war in Iran.

video:video.video:description: Washington Capitals captain and NHL all-time goals leader Alexander Ovechkin discusses his future plans as he considers retirement after 21 seasons.

video:video.video:title: LaMelo Ball apologizes for his role in the injury to Bam Adebayo
video:video.video:description: LaMelo Ball apologized for his role in the injury to Bam Adebayo in Tuesday night’s play-in tournament game but added that he was disoriented after getting hit in the head.

video:video.video:description: The White House unveiled plans Wednesday for Donald Trump’s 250-foot “United States Triumphal Arch,” proposed near the Lincoln Memorial and Arlington National Cemetery.

video:video.video:description: King Charles III will make his first official state visit as monarch to the United States in late April, visiting Washington, D.C., New York and Virginia.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian farming tycoon helps turn tiny volunteer unit into fearsome fighting corps
video:video.video:description: When a Ukrainian agricultural tycoon founded a volunteer unit of 30 people in the early days of Russia’s invasion, he had no certainty he would live to see what came next — but he did, and so did the force he created.

video:video.video:title: Long lines at cannabis stores to celebrate 4/20
video:video.video:description: April 20 marks marijuana culture’s high holiday, the unofficial holiday for cannabis.

video:video.video:description: As Israeli restrictions on entry of goods and commodities into Gaza Strip continue, carpenters have resorted to recycling used wood and pallets to serve a community, stricken by two years of war between Hamas and Israel.

video:video.video:description: Ten years after Prince's death, his first wife Mayte Jannell Garcia has relaunched the Live 4 Love charity that she and the pop star first established after the 1996 death of their infant child.

video:video.video:description: The ceasefire brokered by Pakistan still faces hurdles in the talks beginning Saturday, as Israel and Hezbollah militants have been trading fire along the border of southern Lebanon and Iran has set conditions before negotiations can begin.

video:video.video:description: Thunderstorms tore through parts of Texas on Tuesday, sending at least two people to the hospital as powerful winds ripped roofs off homes, flattened buildings and tossed debris through the air.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse video shows movement of ships in and around the Strait of Hormuz over 24 hours
video:video.video:description: When the United States and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, Iran retaliated with attacks that effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court voids majority Black congressional district in Louisiana, boosting Republican chances
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court has struck down Louisiana’s second majority Black congressional district in a decision that could open the door for Republican-led states to eliminate Black and Latino electoral districts that tend to favor Democrats.

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway turns pop star

video:video.video:description: Anitta says her new album is a vibrant mix of funk, samba, bossa nova and more, showcasing her Brazilian roots and celebrating diversity and unity in a pop package The album also features a collab with superstar Shakira.

video:video.video:title: Samsung workers rally in South Korea, demanding higher bonuses and threatening strike
video:video.video:description: Thousands of unionized workers from Samsung Electronics rallied Thursday at its semiconductor complex in Pyeongtaek, South Korea, demanding higher bonuses and threatening to strike as the booming demand for artificial intelligence drives up memory-chip profits.

video:video.video:title: Trump brushes off war crimes concerns as he widens threat to Iran's power plants and bridges
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday he’s “not at all” concerned about committing possible war crimes as he threatened to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants if Tehran does not meet his Tuesday 8 p.m.

video:video.video:title: Oil plunges below $95 as the Dow surges 1,100 in a worldwide rally following a ceasefire with Iran
video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 leaped 2.5% after Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, less than 90 minutes before a deadline Trump had set for it to open the Strait of Hormuz and allow oil tankers to exit the Persian Gulf.

video:video.video:title: DNA testing links teen's 1974 murder to serial killer Ted Bundy
video:video.video:description: Utah authorities say new DNA testing has definitively linked the unsolved death of a Utah teenager in 1974 to the infamous serial killer Ted Bundy.

video:video.video:title: Universities pressured to strip names of Epstein associates from campus buildings
video:video.video:description: Protests are growing at universities where buildings bear the names of associates of the late sexual predator Jeffrey Epstein.

video:video.video:description: The U.S. on Wednesday lifted sanctions on Venezuela’s acting President Delcy Rodríguez, according to an Office of Foreign Assets Control entry on the Treasury Department website.

video:video.video:title: Solar growth in China and India powers clean energy surge by 2025
video:video.video:description: Record growth in solar, especially in China and India, was a driving factor for clean energy sources surpassing the world’s strong demand for electricity in 2025, according to a new global power analysis.

video:video.video:description: Roblox faces litigation in other states as prosecutors file lawsuits against social media companies over the role they play in children's lives. (AP Video by Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:description: The academic dean of the school where the pope studied supports the Catholic leader's anti-war comments on Easter, saying they align with gospel teachings.

video:video.video:description: Bohdan Serhiiev, was killed in a Russian drone strike on the central Ukrainian city of Cherkasy earlier in the week.

video:video.video:description: Families and space fans attending a watch party at the San Diego Air & Space Museum cheer on as the first astronauts to visit the moon in more than a half-century splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV welcomed the Harlem Globetrotters to St. Peter’s Square on Wednesday. He was seen attempting one of the team’s classic tricks — spinning their red, white, and blue basketball on a finger — with help from one of the players

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 3-9 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: Marilyn Monroe collection reveals personal life
video:video.video:description: Items from the Marilyn Monroe Icon Collection were on display at a press preview at the Waldorf Astoria New York recently, including personal documents that reveal her private life and one of the last items she signed.

video:video.video:title: Former housemates speak about man charged in Atlanta-area killings
video:video.video:description: Two women who once lived in the same house as a man charged in a string of deadly attacks in the Atlanta suburbs speak out about his behavior prior to his arrest (AP Video: Ron Harris)

video:video.video:title: Iran attacks ship in Strait of Hormuz after Trump indefinitely extends the ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Iran fired on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, damaging the vessel and complicating diplomatic efforts to bring the U.S. and Iran to Pakistan for talks.

video:video.video:title: UN chief says international law is ‘being trampled’ especially in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres is urging a resumption of U.S.-Iran negotiations, a continuation of the ceasefire and the respect for freedom of navigation, including in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Israel Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field
video:video.video:description: Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field

video:video.video:title: Gaza woman scrapes living making and selling dolls to bring some joy to kids in war-ravaged territory
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are using arts and crafts to express their trauma from the war and earn an income, as hopes of returning to normal life remain limited with the territory’s humanitarian needs being overshadowed by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Russia’s Putin meets with Iranian FM Araghchi in Saint Petersburg
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in Saint Petersburg on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Large wildfire burns in Clinch County, Georgia, prompting evacuations
video:video.video:description: Wildfires burning across the southeastern U.S. forced more people to flee Wednesday after destroying nearly 50 homes in Georgia and causing school closings.

video:video.video:title: Pope doubles down on message of peace and unity as Trump's criticism continues
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV on Wednesday doubled down as U.S. President Donald Trump’s criticism showed no sign of letting up, insisting that the message "the world needs to hear today” is one of peace and dialogue.

video:video.video:title: Meet the baby koala hiding in its mom’s pouch at a Florida zoo’s new Outback habitat
video:video.video:description: A zoo in Florida has two reasons to celebrate — the first birth of a koala and a newly renovated habitat for the cuddly creatures is opening to the public.

video:video.video:title: Expectations for talks between Lebanon and Israel as Beirut mourns more than 300 dead
video:video.video:description: The Lebanese capital of Beirut was still reeling Friday from the shock of Israeli attacks two days prior, the deadliest day in the country since the renewed war between Israel and Iranian-backed militant group Hezbollah erupted in March.

video:video.video:title: Tribal gas stations offer a reprieve from high prices during Iran war
video:video.video:description: Junelle Lewis was on the hunt for a reprieve from Seattle-area gas prices driven high by the Iran war when an app on her phone gave her the answer: the Tulalip Reservation north of the city, almost half an hour from her home.

video:video.video:title: Ex-funeral home owner gets 30 years in Colorado corpse abuse case
video:video.video:description: A former Colorado funeral home owner who helped her ex-husband hide nearly 200 decomposing bodies was sentenced to 30 years in prison Friday, in a case that forced the state to clamp down on an industry plagued by repeated scandal and notoriously lax oversight.

video:video.video:title: Rain across northern Michigan heightens concerns of river flooding, dam overflows
video:video.video:description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer has declared a state of emergency at the Cheboygan Lock and Dam Complex as record snowfall in March and the recent rain have elevated water levels.

video:video.video:title: Trump’s Cuba threats revive exile hopes, fears over property claims
video:video.video:description: Among Cuban American exiles and their descendants in Miami, hopes have stirred that 2026 could finally be the year of regime change on the communist-run island, and some hope that their property confiscated after the Cuban revolution could be returned if a new government comes to power.

video:video.video:title: New breed of humanoid robots trained to maintain China's power grid
video:video.video:description: Humanoid robots are being trained to carry out maintenance on China’s power grid. Developers say the machines could eventually handle repetitive and hazardous work.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife convicted of attempted manslaughter
video:video.video:description: Hawaii doctor accused of trying to kill his wife convicted of attempted manslaughter

video:video.video:title: Leaders vote in Bulgaria's national elections
video:video.video:description: The election could bring to power a left-leaning ex-president just days after Hungarian voters rejected the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement of Viktor Orbán in Hungary.

video:video.video:title: A robot is beating human pros at table tennis. Its maker calls it a milestone for machines
video:video.video:description: A paddle-wielding robot is so adept at playing table tennis that it is posing a tough challenge to elite human players and sometimes defeating them, according to a new study that shows how advances in artificial intelligence are making robots more agile.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian Prime Minister Orbán is ejected after 16 years in a European electoral earthquake
video:video.video:description: Hungarian voters have ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions.

video:video.video:title: Israeli authorities recover bodies from building hit by Iranian missile
video:video.video:description: Israeli rescuers on Monday removed bodies of people from the rubble of a building hit by an Iranian missile in Haifa on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Crowds revel at cherry blossoms in Tokyo as they near full bloom
video:video.video:description: Cherry blossoms blanket parks in Tokyo as they near full bloom, drawing large crowds of tourists and local residents to revel at the pastel pink flowers.

video:video.video:title: World's oldest tortoise very much alive despite death rumors
video:video.video:description: Jonathan, believed to be 193, is still kicking — albeit slowly — on the island of St. Helena. “It was a hoax,” Anne Dillon, head of communications on the island, told The Associated Press on Thursday about his alleged passing.

video:video.video:description: Two paramedics helped deliver a baby on a Delta flight as it landed in Portland, Oregon. Ashley Blair gave birth to her daughter on Friday night.

video:video.video:description: After a six-year hiatus, BookCon 2026 is back to New York and bringing literature lovers together. (April 19)

video:video.video:description: Kenyan distance runner Sabastian Sawe pulled off a feat that was long considered unthinkable, winning the London Marathon on Sunday in a time of 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds.

video:video.video:description: A suicide bomber rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into a police station in the Bannu district of northwestern Pakistan late Thursday, killing at least five people and wounding several others, local police chief Rafi Ullah said.

video:video.video:description: Following the revelation in the Guardian newspaper, the UK government said Prime Minister Keir Starmer was not aware the Foreign Office had overruled a security vetting process for Peter Mandelson to become UK ambassador to Washington “until earlier this week.”

video:video.video:description: Hundreds of mourners marched on Thursday behind the coffins of three Lebanese Civil Defense paramedics killed by Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon.

video:video.video:description: Haiti, the most impoverished country in the Western Hemisphere, has been hit the hardest by rising oil prices that experts warn will deepen a spiraling humanitarian crisis.

video:video.video:description: A fire has broken out at an Australian oil refinery, further reducing the nation’s fuel supply already threatened by the Iran war.

video:video.video:description: Along the Magdalena River, one of Colombia’s primary waterways, fishermen move with wary precision as hippos introduced by Pablo Escobar in the 1980s have become a major safety issue.

video:video.video:description: The leaders of France and the U.K. will gather countries from Europe and beyond – but not the United States – on Friday to push forward plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil route choked off by the U.S.

video:video.video:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent warns that the US is preparing to ramp up economic pain on Iran by levying secondary sanctions on financial institutions that do business with Tehran.

video:video.video:description: Lindsey Vonn is still recovering physically and emotionally from her frightening crash at the Winter Olympics. if the 41-year-old Vonn races again — and she’s not ready to make that decision — she thinks a return is at least a year and a half away.

video:video.video:description: Ten Republicans, many from districts with large numbers of Haitian residents, joined all Democrats and one independent in voting for the bill.

video:video.video:description: Iran state media reported that a US strike on the B1 bridge killed at least eight people and wounded 95.

video:video.video:description: Severe shortages of water, power, and money, combined with a U.S energy blockade have deepened poverty and increased hunger across Cuba as severe blackouts persist.

video:video.video:description: Singer D4vd has been arrested on suspicion of killing a 14-year-old girl whose decomposed body was found last year in his apparently abandoned Tesla that was towed from the Hollywood Hills, authorities said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Chernobyl's radioactive landscape testament to nature’s resilience
video:video.video:description: Four decades on, Chernobyl remains too dangerous for humans. But the wildlife has moved back in. Wolves now prowl the vast no-man’s-land spanning Ukraine and Belarus, and brown bears have returned after more than a century.

video:video.video:description: A Long Island architect who led a secret life as a serial killer pleads guilty to murdering seven women and admitted he killed an eighth in a string of long-unsolved crimes

video:video.video:title: Richard Gadd turned down job offers to make 'Half Man'
video:video.video:description: Hollywood came calling after the success of Gadd's series "Baby Reindeer." (April 27)

video:video.video:title: Philippine President Marcos debunks health rumors with jumping jacks
video:video.video:description: An annoyed Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. did a few rounds of jumping jack exercise and jogged briefly outside his office in front of journalists Monday to disprove rumors of his failing health.

video:video.video:title: UN warns about Mexico's plight of disappearances
video:video.video:description: Mexico faces a prolonged crisis of disappearances that has pushed families to become investigators, searching for traces of loved ones in clandestine burial sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II engine firing was 'flawless,' NASA says
video:video.video:description: NASA’s Artemis II astronauts fired their engines and blazed toward the moon Thursday night, breaking free of Earth orbit for the first time since 1972.

video:video.video:title: US troops complete their withdrawal from an air base in Syria
video:video.video:description: U.S. forces completed their withdrawal from Qasrak Air Base in Syria's Hasakah province Thursday evening, with the final convoy of soldiers and equipment leaving the site.

video:video.video:title: Inside an LA Justin Bieber look-alike contest
video:video.video:description: Fans gathered in West Hollywood for a Justin Bieber look-alike contest during Coachella weekend, and AP was there. Take a look inside.

video:video.video:title: James McAvoy comes clean about lying for an audition
video:video.video:description: James McAvoy makes his directorial debut with "California Schemin'" - a true story about a rap duo from Dundee who pretended to be American while seeking success - something that McAvoy can relate to after trying on various accents to secure film roles.

video:video.video:title: Video shows Tiger Woods being arrested after Florida crash
video:video.video:description: Newly released video shows Tiger Woods hiccuping and appearing to nod off in the back of a patrol car after crashing his vehicle last week in Florida.

video:video.video:title: Unmanned cargo spacecraft takes off for International Space Station
video:video.video:description: The unmanned Progress 95 took off on board a Soyuz rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The Roscosmos spacecraft carries food, fuel, and supplies for the crew on board the International Space Station.

video:video.video:title: Fashionistas rejoice: Devil Wears Prada returns to Milan's big screens
video:video.video:description: As fashionistas gear up for the return of the Devil Wears Prada to cinemas, the buzz has hit Milan. The Italian city is the birthplace of the luxury designer brand immortalized in the movie's title.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents picnic in parks on last day of Nowruz despite airstrikes on country
video:video.video:description: The annual public picnic day, called Sizdeh Bedar, which comes from the Farsi words for “thirteen” and “day out,” is a legacy from Iran’s pre-Islamic past that hard-liners in the Islamic Republic never managed to erase from calendars.

video:video.video:title: Why Cameron Diaz doesn't Google herself
video:video.video:description: Cameron Diaz stars in new dark comedy "Outcome" - a film about fame and cancel culture. In a recent interview with The Associated Press, she reflects on her own experience of living life in the spotlight.

video:video.video:title: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived at the White House Monday for a four-day U.S. visit celebrating America’s 250th anniversary.
video:video.video:tag: George III of the United Kingdom

video:video.video:title: Dave Chappelle stands firm through backlash and considers revisiting ‘Chappelle’s Show’
video:video.video:description: Dave Chappelle finds clarity away from the spotlight in the Ohio village where he lives. In an interview, he reflects on his comedic voice before the opening of a restored 19th-century schoolhouse that now houses a public radio station.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth slams Iran war critics as the 'biggest adversary' in heated hearing
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth lashed out at lawmakers who have criticized the Iran war, calling them the “biggest adversary we face,” as he defended the conflict before a heated House Armed Services Committee hearing Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Closely divided Congress reacts to Virginia redistricting result that could help Democrats
video:video.video:description: Republicans and Democrats in a closely divided Congress are reacting to Virginia voters approving a mid-decade redistricting plan that could boost Democrats’ chances of winning four additional U.S.

video:video.video:title: What legacy has Queen Elizabeth II left, 100 years after her birth?
video:video.video:description: April 21 marks 100 years since the birth of the late Queen Elizabeth II. She was the UK's longest serving monarch, but what kind of legacy did she leave behind?

video:video.video:title: Israel's main airport resumes full operations as Iran ceasefire holds despite friction
video:video.video:description: Israel’s main airport resumed full operations on Thursday after the Iran war stranded tens of thousands of people, including both Israelis abroad and visiting tourists.

video:video.video:title: Eyes are on the Bab el-Mandeb Strait after the closure of the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday made expletive-filled threats against Iran and its infrastructure if it doesn't open the Strait of Hormuz by his Tuesday deadline, after American forces rescued a wounded aviator whose Iran-downed plane fell behind enemy lines.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to a 10-day ceasefire starting Thursday
video:video.video:description: Israel and Lebanon opened negotiations this week in Washington aimed at forging a peace agreement. President Donald Trump announced that he will invite both leaders to meet at the White House.

video:video.video:title: Displaced relieved at Lebanon ceasefire but shocked by extent of destruction as they return home
video:video.video:description: A 10-day truce appeared to be holding in Lebanon early Friday, promising a pause in fighting between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group and possibly clearing one major obstacle to a deal between Iran and the United States and Israel to end weeks of devastating war.

video:video.video:title: China company offers glimpse into how AI could help reduce impact of synthetic textile waste
video:video.video:description: In an industrial park in Zhangjiagang, a small city on China's east coast, a large humming and hissing machine feeds on piles of used clothes and sorts them.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage released as London police say stabbing of two Jewish men was act of terrorism
video:video.video:description: London's Metropolitan Police on Wednesday released video from the body-worn cameras of two officers involved in detaining a man accused of stabbing two Jewish men on a London street in the city's latest antisemitic attack.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán concedes defeat after 'painful' election result
video:video.video:description: Hungarian voters have ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions.

video:video.video:title: Election loss for Hungarian Prime Minister Orbán has ripple effects for Trump, US conservatives
video:video.video:description: In a stunning blow to Viktor Orban, Hungarian voters have ousted the prime minister after 16 years in power, which could have significant reverberations in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Iran war energy shocks drive nuclear power plans in hard-hit Asia and Africa
video:video.video:description: The energy shock from the Iran war is boosting nuclear power generation among some nations and adding momentum to the atomic desires of hard-hit countries in Africa and Asia.

video:video.video:title: Opposition leader Peter Magyar votes in election that could unseat Orbán after 16 years
video:video.video:description: Hungarian prime-minister Viktor Orbán's top challenger, Péter Magyar voted on Sunday, as Hungarians were casting ballots in what is widely seen as Europe's most consequential election this year, a vote that could unseat populist Orbán, an ally of U.S.

video:video.video:title: As China develops renewable energy, its coal miners are facing the pressure of major change
video:video.video:description: As China prioritizes renewable energy over coal, miners are being forced to confront uncertainty. (AP video shot by: Wayne Zhang)

video:video.video:title: American gas prices remain high amid ongoing Strait of Hormuz closure
video:video.video:description: The Iran war has strained global fuel supplies, as the Strait of Hormuz remains a vital choke point for international trade.

video:video.video:title: Powerful winds and reported tornadoes rip through the Midwest, leaving heavy damage but no deaths
video:video.video:description: A trail of damaged homes and buildings dotted a wide swath of the U.S. on Saturday after a burst of destructive winds and reported tornadoes tore off roofs, uprooted trees and rendered rural roads impassable with debris.

video:video.video:title: Breaking the Ice: 'Heated Rivalry' fuels LGBTQ+ hockey growth
video:video.video:description: The San Francisco Earthquakes are experiencing a surge in interest and attendance, fueled in part by the hit show "Heated Rivalry," which has helped more LGBTQ+ players feel seen in a sometimes unwelcoming sport.

video:video.video:title: Animals killed after Russia strike damages Kyiv region veterinary clinic
video:video.video:description: Animals were killed after a Russian strike damaged a veterinary clinic in the Kyiv region on Friday, the State Emergency Service reported.

video:video.video:title: BeWell: Some young men are discovering the wellbeing benefits of crocheting
video:video.video:description: In Italy some young men are embracing crafts traditionally associated with women, such as crochet, discovering the wellbeing benefits of mindful crafting.

video:video.video:title: Oklahoma governor tours rural town damaged by powerful tornado
video:video.video:description: Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt toured the small rural town of Enid Friday, about 85 miles (about 135 kilometres) north of Oklahoma City, where an EF-4 tornado hit on Thursday night.

video:video.video:title: Two killed in drone attack on civilian house in Irbil, authorities say
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed after an explosive-laden drone launched from Iran struck a civilian home in Irbil province, Iraq early Tuesday, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Trump welcomes Supreme Court Voting Rights Act ruling as the 'kind of ruling I like'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says says more congressional districts should be redrawn after Wednesday's Supreme Court ruling.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II crew share emotional moment over loved one lost after breaking record
video:video.video:description: Moments after breaking Apollo 13’s record, the astronauts asked permission to name two fresh lunar craters already observed.

video:video.video:title: Trump to be featured on commemorative US passports for America 250
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump will appear on commemorative U.S. passports for America’s 250th anniversary, becoming the first living president featured in the document.

video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moon ship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:title: Ships wait in Strait of Hormuz with US and Iran locked in a standoff
video:video.video:description: Two regional officials say that Iran has offered to end its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the U.S. lifting its blockade on the country and an end to the war.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians queue for hours in pouring rain to get bread, amid food shortage in war torn Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are struggling to meet their daily bread needs amid shortages of supplies entering the enclave and a lack of money.

video:video.video:title: A look at life on board an Iraqi commercial oil tanker amid Iran war
video:video.video:description: On board an oil tanker navigating the tense waters between the Gulf of Oman and the Strait of Hormuz, Iraqi Captain Rahman Al-Jubouri continues to work in one of the world’s most volatile maritime corridors, where the U.S.

video:video.video:title: New species of spider captured on video for the first time
video:video.video:description: A new species of spider has been captured on video for the first time. Researchers in southern Spain are racing to learn more about the tiny Cryptodrassus michaeli arachnid and the ecosystem it inhabits.

video:video.video:title: Chester Zoo welcomes the birth of one of world's tiniest antelopes
video:video.video:description: Zookeepers at Chester Zoo said Dotty will reach just 40cm in height when fully grown. The fawn will be raised by a surrogate father after her biological father died of old age shortly before her birth.

video:video.video:title: Iranian FM Araghchi meets Pakistani PM Sharif
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Pakistan's capital of Islamabad on Friday to take part in the Pakistani-led mediation efforts, and held a series of meetings with Pakistani officials.

video:video.video:description: The U.S. military on Tuesday said it boarded a commercial ship suspected of attempting to transit to Iran in violation of the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports.

video:video.video:title: DC mayor says 'no reason to believe' anyone else involved in dinner shooting incident
video:video.video:description: In a news conference on Saturday, Muriel Bowser thanked law enforcement "who did their jobs tonight and made sure that all guests were safe."

video:video.video:title: A growing amateur choir brings joy and community to hundreds in Serbia
video:video.video:description: An amateur pop choir that started with a couple dozen singers in a small Serbian town has found unexpected success across the country. Its motto? Anyone can sing.

video:video.video:title: Miniature art transforms an alley into a surrealist wonderland at "Mississippi's Tiniest Museum"
video:video.video:description: The Hattiesburg Pocket Museum, which started as a way to spread joy during the covid-19 pandemic, has since drawn more than 300,000 visitors eager to explore the alley's often colorful, sometimes hidden displays.

video:video.video:description: Officials say crashes involving about 70 vehicles have bottlenecked a section of a snowy Colorado highway and sent eight people to the hospital.

video:video.video:title: Why wildfires are burning longer than they used to
video:video.video:description: Wildfires used to die down and even stop at night with cooler temperatures and lower humidity. But a study says climate change is making burning weather more around the clock in North America because night is becoming warmer and drier.

video:video.video:title: House extends surveillance powers until April 30 after late-night revolt sinks GOP plan
video:video.video:description: The House has approved a short-term renewal until April 30 of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies.

video:video.video:title: Iraq's oil hub slows to crawl as Strait of Hormuz shutdown strangles exports
video:video.video:description: Iraqi oil fields once alive with the buzz of workers are nearly deserted. Ports that pulsed with the churn of cargo have fallen still, the din of commerce replaced by the soft rhythm of waves.

video:video.video:description: Interest rates affect how much people spend when they borrow to buy a home, car, or large appliance. Yet different forces can affect those rates

video:video.video:title: Rescuers lose hope for humpback whale stranded in Baltic Sea
video:video.video:description: Rescuers said Wednesday that they have lost hope for the humpback whale that has become stranded repeatedly off Germany's Baltic Sea coast and now expect it to die in the inlet where it currently lies.

video:video.video:title: Video shows armed man shooting at tourists at Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City
video:video.video:description: An armed man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacán pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people injured at the archaeological site north of Mexico’s capital, authorities said.

video:video.video:description: Republican and Democratic U.S. Senators weigh in on Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve and replace current Fed chair Jerome Powell.

video:video.video:title: Pope in Cameroon's separatist west says 'world is being ravaged by a handful of tyrants'
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV blasted those he claimed were "ravaging" Earth with war and exploitation, as he preached a message of peace Thursday in the epicenter of a separatist conflict considered one of the world’s most neglected crises.

video:video.video:title: Fresh starters attend underwater welcoming ceremony in western Japan
video:video.video:description: First of April marks the start of a new fiscal and academic year in Japan, a time widely regarded as a fresh start.

video:video.video:title: Iranian minister dismisses Trump threat in AP interview
video:video.video:description: Iran’s culture minister on Sunday dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest threats, calling him an “unstable, delusional figure. (AP video shot by Bassam Hatoum)

video:video.video:description: Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter said Tuesday the first negotiation talks between Israel and Lebanon were "positive", saying about the two countries "we are on the same side of the equation."

video:video.video:description: Iran's representative to the U.N. said Tehran would “take immediate and proportionate” action if President Donald Trump follows through on his threats to attack the country’s “whole civilization” if Iran does not make a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moonship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:description: Leaders of major media companies are urging Israel to lift a ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza.

video:video.video:description: New video when U.S. Navy divers reach and open the Orion capsule of Artemis II reaching the astronauts for the first time in 10 days after their trip to the moon.

video:video.video:description: Explosions were heard and a column of smoke was seen rising in Tehran early on Wednesday following the latest Israel-U.S. strike on the Iranian capital.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 22
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 22nd: Iran fires on 3 ships in the Strait of Hormuz; Pope Leo XIV visits Equatorial Guinea; Warner shareholders set to vote on Paramount's $81 billion takeover; Sony's AI robot is beating human pros at table tennis.

video:video.video:title: Stay tuned Cincinnati: WKRP is coming to town for real, North Carolina station's director says
video:video.video:description: In a case of life imitating art, there could soon be a real "WKRP in Cincinnati." A North Carolina nonprofit that has used the famous call letters for a low-power radio broadcast has signed a deal to let a group launch a station in the Ohio city that was home to the fictional station from the popula

video:video.video:title: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts return home to cheers after a record-breaking trip
video:video.video:description: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts are back home and feted to a thunderous welcome. Still marveling over their record-breaking lunar fly-around, the crew of four flew to Houston's Ellington Field from San Diego on Saturday afternoon.

video:video.video:description: A long-lasting weather pattern is blasting hot air across the eastern United States. The heat wave threatens to shatter temperatures records Wednesday in cities like New York, Philadelphia, and Washington, D.C.

video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he thinks the war in Iran is 'very close to over.' Trump commented in an interview with Maria Bartiromo of Fox Business News.

video:video.video:title: Happy 69th birthday to Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla living in captivity
video:video.video:description: The world’s oldest gorilla living in captivity has celebrated her 69th birthday. Fatou spent Monday munching on cherry tomatoes, beets, leeks and lettuce at the Berlin Zoo (AP video shot by Fanny Brodersen).

video:video.video:title: Digital detox: welcome to the offline club for those wanting a screen-free connection
video:video.video:description: A new kind of club has emerged in Amsterdam - one where participants choose to go offline, away from their phones and other devices. (AP video by Aleksandar Furtula)

video:video.video:description: Britain celebrates late Queen Elizabeth II as a style icon to mark her centenary

video:video.video:description: The attacks in Lebanon came hours after a ceasefire was announced in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

video:video.video:description: Two trains collided in Denmark early Thursday, prompting a massive emergency response for what police called a major accident.

video:video.video:title: Mother of killed Gaza hostage on loss of her son and how she navigates grief
video:video.video:description: When Hersh Goldberg-Polin was in the tunnels in Gaza after being kidnapped from a music festival, released hostages recalled he often quoted a line from Holocaust survivor Viktor Frankl: “Those who have a ‘why’ to live, can bear with almost any ‘how.’”

video:video.video:description: At least 14 people were killed when an explosive device detonated as a bus passed by on a highway in southwestern Colombia on Saturday.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump confirmed Monday a U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports is underway, saying “we can’t let a country blackmail” the world.

video:video.video:title: You aren't the only one who just sits in the car before or after a long day
video:video.video:description: Pausing to decompress in a parked car can help you reset, if you keep a few things in mind. That temporary pause can act as a buffer between one part of the day and the next.

video:video.video:description: An airstrike hit Monday an information and communication technology building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology, a professor there said.

video:video.video:description: The near-daily changes in U.S. gas prices have been dizzying for drivers. Experts say differences in price aren’t typically decided by any individual gas retailer, and most of them aren't pocketing the extra pennies when prices rise.

video:video.video:title: Brian Cox reveals his feelings about turning 80
video:video.video:description: Brian Cox says he's never been aware of his age — until he realized he was turning 80 on June 1. The "Succession" star's directorial debut is "Glenrothan."

video:video.video:title: Utility bills are exceeding mortgages in West Virginia despite Trump’s promised cuts
video:video.video:description: President Trump promised to cut electricity bills, but prices have risen instead. Many in West Virginia now face utility costs surpassing rents and mortgages.

video:video.video:title: Could more cattle cause record beef prices to drop? Ranchers say it's not that simple
video:video.video:description: It’s never been so expensive for Americans to buy a steak or hamburger, but cutting those costs requires ranchers like to raise more cattle — and that's not an easy ask.

video:video.video:description: Planning to catch a World Cup match at MetLife Stadium? That quick train ride from New York City could cost you $150 round trip — nearly 12 times the usual fare.

video:video.video:description: Iran's supreme leader vows more defeats for US and Israel at hands of Iranian navy

video:video.video:title: 'My house is gone!' exclaims woman eyeing Georgia wildfire damage
video:video.video:description: A woman recording the damage caused by a wildfire in Brantley County, Georgia cried out, "My house is gone!"

video:video.video:title: Tennessee librarian fired for not relocating LGBTQ books
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee library board has fired the county’s top librarian for refusing to comply with its vote to move more than 100 LGBTQ books from the children’s to the adult section over its claims that they promote “gender confusion.”

video:video.video:title: Good Friday a solemn day for Christians, with ancient prayers and fervent processions
video:video.video:description: Celebrated the Friday before Easter, Good Friday commemorates the crucifixion and death of Jesus. This year, it will fall on April 3 for Catholics and Protestants, and April 10 for Orthodox Christians.

video:video.video:description: An Armenian Orthodox church commemorated on Wednesday the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. (AP video by Bassam Hatoum)

video:video.video:description: While the region is estimated to generate over 3.3 billion in economic activity throughout the tournament, Governor Hochul expressed the importance to ensure the people who live in New York “don't feel overlooked”.

video:video.video:description: With the moon looming ever larger, the Artemis II astronauts raced to set a new distance record Monday from Earth on a lunar fly-around promising magnificent views of the far side never seen before by eye.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and King Charles III greet each other warmly as the monarch begins a day of diplomacy that started with a White House ceremony.

video:video.video:description: A newly discovered 17th-century map sheds new light on Shakespeare’s London life, pinpointing for the first time the exact location of the only home he bought in the city, and where he may have worked on his final plays.

video:video.video:description: Students are searching for “AI-proof” majors as AI changes the job market — but no one knows what actually counts.

video:video.video:title: Europe has 'maybe 6 weeks of jet fuel left,' energy agency head tells AP
video:video.video:description: Europe has “maybe 6 weeks or so (of) jet fuel left,” the head of the International Energy Agency Fatih Birol said Thursday in a wide-ranging Associated Press interview, warning of possible flight cancellations “soon” if oil supplies remain blocked by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian analyst on 'historical win' for Magyar's Tisza party
video:video.video:description: Hungarian political analyst Bulcsú Hunyadi described Péter Magyar's election victory as "historical," but warned there were "obstacles in the system" facing his Tisza party.

video:video.video:title: New supersized telescope in Atacama desert could offer view of Earth-like planets
video:video.video:description: With more than 300 clear nights a year, Chile's Atacama has become home to the world’s largest ground-based astronomical projects.

video:video.video:title: Merz says the American nation “is being humiliated” by the Iranian leadership
video:video.video:description: The German Chancellor said on Monday that U.S. ‘is being humiliated’ by the Iranian leadership, and criticized America for going into the Iran war without any strategy, saying this also makes it harder to end the conflict.

video:video.video:title: Floodwaters inundate Wisconsin streets, trapping drivers
video:video.video:description: Floodwaters from record rainfall in Wisconsin inundated streets Wednesday, trapping drivers and forcing officials to close sections of a highway on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Shopkeepers worried about impact of 'smart lockdown' over fuel price rises
video:video.video:description: Pakistan’s federal government has ordered markets and shopping malls to close in the country by 8:00 p.m., as a fuel crisis deepens in the region.

video:video.video:title: Fire in Malaysian Borneo water village destroys about 1,000 homes, displaces thousands
video:video.video:description: A fire that tore through a Malaysian coastal settlement on Borneo Island destroyed about 1,000 homes and displaced over 9,000 people, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Fed Chair Powell warns of 'unprecedented' legal attacks on Fed by Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Jerome Powell said Wednesday he plans to remain on the board of the Federal Reserve after his term as chair ends next month.

video:video.video:title: Senate Banking Committee quizzes Fed nominee Warsh over economy, independence
video:video.video:description: Senators on Tuesday quizzed President Donald Trump’s nominee to chair the Federal Reserve about whether he is able to stay independent of the president.

video:video.video:title: Asia markets react to the failure of U.S.-Iran talks
video:video.video:description: Trading in Asia was weighed down Monday by the failure of the United States and Iran to reach a peace agreement. (AP Video: Johnson Lai and Yong Jun Chang)

video:video.video:title: In Tennessee, solar developers explore benefits of cattle and energy production
video:video.video:description: A solar company is testing how cattle and solar panels can better co-exist at a small farm in central Tennessee and what the potential benefits might be for farmers, the energy industry and the land.

video:video.video:title: Nissan launches Infiniti QX65, a luxury SUV, at its Tennessee plant
video:video.video:description: Nissan is launching a new luxury SUV, the Infiniti QX65, out of its Smyrna, Tennessee plant, as auto manufacturers deal with lingering issues with tariffs and affordability.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents react to peace talks ending without agreement
video:video.video:description: Some Tehran residents on Sunday were in favor of diplomacy despite high-level talks with the United States ending without an agreement to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices rocket higher and stocks tumble after Trump’s address
video:video.video:description: Stocks slumped and oil prices soared Thursday after President Donald Trump vowed the U.S. will continue to attack Iran and failed to offer a clear timetable for ending the conflict in the Middle East.

video:video.video:title: 'The Testaments' stars hope their show inspires like 'Handmaid's'
video:video.video:description: Hulu's "The Testaments" - starring Lucy Halliday and Chase Infiniti - is a sequel to hit dystopian drama "The Handmaid's Tale."

video:video.video:title: Rwanda tries to protect farmland in Africa’s most densely populated nation
video:video.video:description: Since September, Rwanda’s government has been mapping agricultural land and using satellite imagery to track any development encroaching on farmlands and forests in a country where the population is expected to reach 22 million in a couple of years.

video:video.video:title: Japan's Kabuki crown passes to a new generation
video:video.video:description: In Kabuki, Japan's traditional theater art dating back to the 1600s, passing down a name across generations is a sacred ritual.

video:video.video:title: Local affiliate of Iran state TV claims a US fighter pilot ejected from an aircraft in southwest Iran
video:video.video:description: The anchor on the channel read the following announcement: “If you capture the enemy pilot or pilots alive and hand them over to the police, you will receive a precious prize.”

video:video.video:title: Family of missing Navajo woman still waiting for answers
video:video.video:description: On June 15, 2021, Ella Mae Begay's truck was seen leaving her Sweetwater, Arizona home before dawn. Five years later, the missing woman's family is still seeking answers.

video:video.video:title: Wildfire burns in South Florida as the state faces one of its worst fire seasons in decades
video:video.video:description: Firefighters in Florida are battling 131 wildfires that have burned 34 square miles. A long drought, low humidity, and strong winds are fueling the fires.

video:video.video:title: Inside the classic car junkyard hidden in a US forest
video:video.video:description: What if a junkyard could turn into an open-air forest museum? Old Car City is home to more than 4,400 classic American-made cars, including the last one Elvis Presley bought.

video:video.video:description: Two years ago, Dr. Mohammed Ziara watched Israel ravage Gaza's health care system, shelling hospitals, striking ambulances and forcing patients to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Building damaged in town of Ramat Gan after Iran fires cluster munition towards Israel
video:video.video:description: Rescue teams worked to clear the debris from a damaged home in the central town of Ramat Gan on Saturday after Iran fired what the Israeli army said was a cluster munition towards Israel.

video:video.video:title: Humanoid robot chases wild boars in the Polish capital Warsaw

video:video.video:description: A watch built for space is back in the spotlight in Geneva. Omega’s Speedmaster line has been worn by astronauts for decades and is again drawing attention after being seen on the wrists of the crew of Artemis II.

video:video.video:description: Brazil’s President Lula da Silva is boosting his reelection bid at age 80 by showcasing his fitness. Critics say his workouts are more popular than he is.

video:video.video:title: Massachusetts weather station shows the science behind our climate
video:video.video:description: At Blue Hill Observatory and Science Center near Boston, staff and volunteers have been using many of the same tools to monitor the weather since it opened in 1885.

video:video.video:description: Russia's Daniil Medvedev had a racket smashing moment in his 6-0, 6-0 defeat at Monte-Carlo Masters on Wednesday.

video:video.video:description: A New Orleans company has created a system that turns glass bottles into sand to help fight Louisiana's land loss crisis. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:description: As the fuel crisis in Cuba continues, the island's residents are switching up how they travel. (AP shot by Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes security incident at White House correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump was unharmed and other top White House officials were evacuated from an annual dinner of the White House Correspondents' Association after a man armed with guns and knives stormed the lobby and opened fire.

video:video.video:title: How biometrics are revolutionising EU travel
video:video.video:description: A new digital border system set to replace passport stamping across much of Europe will be fully implemented from 10 April 2026.

video:video.video:title: London police say stabbing of 2 Jewish men has been declared a terrorist incident
video:video.video:description: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism.

video:video.video:title: Designer Easter treats in stunning colours and shapes
video:video.video:description: A chocolatier in Barcelona has created high-end traditional Catalan treats for Easter. Known as 'monas', they can come in a variety of shapes and flavours.

video:video.video:title: Escaped wolf captured and returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search
video:video.video:description: Escaped wolf captured and returned to South Korean zoo after nine-day search

video:video.video:title: South Lebanon residents flee amid Israeli warning to residents of Nabatieh province
video:video.video:description: People fled different villages in southern Lebanon on Sunday amid Israeli warnings to residents of villages in the Nabatieh province.

video:video.video:title: Artemis crew saw things they 'had never even imagined'
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II mission crew held a news conference from on board the Orion spacecraft on Wednesday, as Canadian Space Agency (CSA) astronaut Jeremy Hansen said he saw things he "had never even imagined."

video:video.video:title: Mounted police officer and his horse corner suspected purse snatcher in New York
video:video.video:description: A suspected purse snatcher was rounded up after being chased through the streets of Manhattan by a mounted New York police officer and his horse.

video:video.video:title: Georgia officials urge residents near wildfires to remain ready to evacuate
video:video.video:description: A wildfire fueled by shifting winds scorched homes and vehicles in southeastern Georgia Thursday, forcing residents to decide whether to evacuate or stay behind.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts speak with President Trump after moon flyby
video:video.video:description: The four astronauts on board the Artemis II spoke with President Donald Trump on after their record-breaking trip around the moon.

video:video.video:title: Why Jonah Hill chose Keanu Reeves to be canceled in 'Outcome'
video:video.video:description: Director Jonah Hill reveals why he cast Keanu Reeves in his new dark comedy, "Outcome," as an actor struggling to keep his head above water bwhen being blackmailed over a compromising video.

video:video.video:title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of girl, 14, found dismembered in his car
video:video.video:description: Singer D4vd has been charged with the murder of a 14-year-old girl who authorities say was found dismembered in his Tesla last year.

video:video.video:title: Clouded leopard cub is already making adorable squeaks at the Nashville Zoo
video:video.video:description: Nashville Zoo is celebrating a conservation milestone following the birth of a female clouded leopard cub, the 50th born at the zoo since 1991.

video:video.video:title: Bodycam footage shows Tiger Woods being arrested
video:video.video:description: Bodycam video shows Tiger Woods being placed in hand cuffs on suspicion of driving under the influence after his vehicle crashed in Florida on March 27th.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, April 5: Trump threatens Iran over the Strait of Hormuz.; Israeli‑Lebanese border tensions high; Artemis II aims to go farther from Earth than Apollo 13; Pope Leo XIV calls for peace during his first Easter Mass.

video:video.video:title: CCTV footage appears to show kidnapping of US journalist Shelly Kittleson in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist who has worked for years in Iraq and Syria, remains missing after her abduction on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Woman escapes from police car while handcuffed
video:video.video:description: A woman who shimmied out the rear window of a police cruiser while handcuffed and then fled over the weekend was arrested Tuesday, authorities in western Michigan said.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 8
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, April 8th: US, Israel and Iran agree to tentative ceasefire; Israeli strikes central Beirut, killing scores ; Rex Heuermann pleads guilty in the Gilgo Beach killings; Charles and Camilla celebrate 21st wedding anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Beirut neighborhood hit overnight in fatal Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed in an Israeli strike early Wednesday in Beirut's Jnah neighborhood.

video:video.video:title: Mike Myers says different generations enjoying his work is 'all a dream'
video:video.video:description: Mike Myers says it’s “very, very satisfying” to hear from audiences who are still discovering — and revisiting — his work across generations.

video:video.video:title: Archaeologists reconstruct the face of a man fleeing the Pompeii eruption using AI
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists at the ancient Roman site of Pompeii have used artificial intelligence to digitally reconstruct the face of a victim of the AD 79 eruption of Mount Vesuvius.

video:video.video:title: Hot air balloon with 13 aboard makes emergency landing in California backyard
video:video.video:description: A balloon landed in a Southern California backyard — a balloon with 13 people. The enormous hot air balloon, with a pilot and passengers in the basket, descended perfectly Saturday on a small plot of grass at a home in Temecula.

video:video.video:title: Skeptical Democrats confront Hegseth about Iran war for the first time since conflict started
video:video.video:description: A hearing before the House Armed Services Committee is focused on the Trump administration's $1.5 trillion military budget for 2027, but Democrats are using the opportunity to grill the Secretary of Defense on the Iran war’s costs.

video:video.video:title: Preserving rural Jewish life is the focus of a Maine center this Passover
video:video.video:description: The Center for Small Town Jewish Life at Colby College is growing as Passover ends this year. The center began a decade ago with a goal of supporting Jewish congregations that are far from big cities.

video:video.video:title: Former Migos rapper Offset shot outside a Florida casino
video:video.video:description: Offset, a former member of the influential hip-hop trio Migos, was shot outside a Florida casino and is in stable condition, a spokesperson said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump signs order to speed review of psychedelics, including the controversial drug ibogaine
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Saturday directed his administration to speed up reviews of certain psychedelic drugs, including ibogaine, which recently has been embraced by combat veterans and conservative lawmakers despite having serious safety risks.

video:video.video:title: Francis Mauigoa represents Samoan culture at the NFL Draft
video:video.video:description: Francis Mauigoa is representing Samoan culture at the NFL Draft.

video:video.video:title: One killed and more than a dozen injured in train crash at level crossing in Northern France
video:video.video:description: A high-speed train driver was killed on Tuesday in northern France when his locomotive collided into a truck at a level crossing in Northern France.

video:video.video:title: Tanker truck in Texas erupts into fiery blaze after collision
video:video.video:description: A tanker truck in Texas hauling gasoline erupted into a fiery blaze on Sunday morning after colliding with another vehicle and knocking over power lines, leaving the truck driver in critical condition.

video:video.video:title: Stampede at Haitian mountaintop fortress leaves at least 25 dead and dozens injured
video:video.video:description: A stampede at a mountaintop fortress popular with tourists in northern Haiti has left at least 25 people dead and several dozen others injured.

video:video.video:title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
video:video.video:description: Judges at the International Criminal Court on Thursday confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte for deadly anti-drugs crackdowns he allegedly oversaw while in office.

video:video.video:title: Why 'unretired' seniors are picking up gig work to pay the bills
video:video.video:description: A growing number of Americans have “unretired” in recent years. After ending careers that spanned decades, they returned to the workforce due to rising living costs, insufficient retirement savings, and a desire to stay active.

video:video.video:description: Sabastian Sawe of Kenya makes history, becoming the first to run a record-eligible marathon in under two hours, winning the London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes and 30 seconds.

video:video.video:description: Beth Benike says technical problems have stymied efforts to collect $50,000 in tariff refunds she paid to get her baby mat products into the country.

video:video.video:description: Police officers arrived at an address in Torrance, California, connected to Cole Tomas Allen, the shooting suspect at the White House correspondents' dinner attended by President Donald Trump on Saturday night.

video:video.video:description: U.S. carrier JetBlue has raised its checked bag fees by as much as $9 as the war in the Middle East disrupts global oil supplies and increases fuel prices.

video:video.video:description: Iranian-American Ali Khaleghi talked to his family in Tehran two weeks ago and said they were anxious, not because of the war going on in their country, but because the Iranian regime is still there.

video:video.video:description: The skies over the Greek island of Crete took on Martian hues on Wednesday. The eerie spectacle was the result of strong winds blowing stifling dust across the Mediterranean from North Africa.

video:video.video:description: Zendaya strutted down the carpet on Hollywood Boulevard to celebrate "Euphoria's" season three premiere in Los Angeles, Tuesday. (April 7)

video:video.video:title: A native seabird population is surging in Honolulu, finding a home among office towers and traffic
video:video.video:description: Manu-o-Kū, a seabird native to Hawaii, is flourishing in the middle of Honolulu's concrete towers, traffic-clogged roads and Waikiki hotels, defying the fate of many other native birds in Hawaii.

video:video.video:description: Hossein Baninajar, an Iranian American who has lived in the U.S. for 11 years, said he feared losing his mother and sister if President Donald Trump had followed through on a threat to destroy Iran’s civilization Tuesday night.

video:video.video:description: Migrants in Spain are facing hours-long lines and in some cases are sleeping on the streets overnight for a chance at the country's "extraordinary regularization" scheme.

video:video.video:description: It's been a month since Hezbollah fired rockets into Israel after the U.S.-Israeli attack on its patron, Iran, triggering Israeli bombardment of Lebanon and a ground invasion.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's interior ministry investigating shooting in Kyiv
video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in the streets of Kyiv Saturday before taking hostages and barricading himself in a nearby supermarket has been shot dead by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans celebrates Easter Sunday with a rainy French Quarter Parade
video:video.video:description: Parade-goers traveled to the historic streets of the French Quarter in New Orleans for an annual Easter Sunday parade in the rain. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:title: Mississippi middle school students stop bus from crashing after driver blacks out
video:video.video:description: After their bus driver blacked out during an asthma attack, five Mississippi middle schoolers worked together to stop the bus, call for help and give their bus driver her medication.

video:video.video:title: Robots crack jokes and grasp items at Humanoid Robot Expo in Tokyo
video:video.video:description: They can dance and they're good at doing repetitive work we humans tire of, but the big question at the Tokyo Expo is when will the humanoids be able to look after us at home.

video:video.video:description: Corgi dogs were racing against each other in an Olympic-style event in Prague in the Czech Republic on Saturday. The fastest record was 8.9 seconds for a 100-metre race.

video:video.video:title: How Pakistan has emerged as a mediator between US and Iran
video:video.video:description: It was initially seen as an unexpected mediator, but this week Pakistan has established itself as a key player in bringing Iran and the United States to the negotiating table.

video:video.video:title: Young voters in Hungary played crucial role in ending Orbán's 16-year rule
video:video.video:description: On Sunday, young voters celebrated the victory of pro-European candidate Péter Magyar. Polls showed 65% of voters under 30 supported Magyar before the vote.

video:video.video:title: Myanmar's ruling general elected as President, keeping army in charge
video:video.video:description: Myanmar’s parliament on Friday elected Min Aung Hlaing, a general who ousted Aung San Suu Kyi’s civilian government in 2021 and kept an iron grip on power for the past five years, as the country’s new president.

video:video.video:title: Jeanie Buss on the rise of women in sports
video:video.video:description: Jeanie Buss talks the rise of women in sports at the "Running Point" premiere. The Netflix series is loosely based on her life as CEO of the Los Angeles Lakers.

video:video.video:title: Meet the Liberty Bell fans visiting little-known replicas scattered across the country
video:video.video:description: For a 1950 bond drive, the U.S. Treasury Department commissioned replicas for each U.S. state and several territories, and there's a group of bell hunters out there trying to visit and ring as many as they can.

video:video.video:title: Massive warehouse fire in southern California; one person arrested

video:video.video:title: Louisiana GOP races to scrap elected office won by a man exonerated after 30 years in prison
video:video.video:description: Calvin Duncan has been elected as New Orleans' new clerk of criminal court, but now Republican lawmakers in Baton Rouge are racing to eliminate the position.

video:video.video:description: The remains of three Indonesian United Nations peacekeepers who were killed while on escort duty supporting U.N. operations in southern Lebanon arrived in Jakarta on Saturday evening, where President Prabowo Subianto led a solemn welcome ceremony.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said Tuesday that U.S. President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy Iranian civilization was “truly unacceptable” and said any attacks on civilian infrastructure violate international law.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump threatened to blow up every bridge and power plant in Iran, action that would be so far-reaching that some experts in military law said it could constitute a war crime.

video:video.video:description: A proposed constitutional amendment would bypass the state's bipartisan redistricting commission to allow use of new congressional districts approved by state lawmakers in this year's midterm elections.

video:video.video:description: European Union leaders meeting in Cyprus need to start preparing a playbook on what should happen if an EU country under attack puts out a call for help from bloc partners, the president of Cyprus said.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived Tuesday in Equatorial Guinea on the fourth and final leg of his Africa journey.

video:video.video:description: Ajay Haridasse was close to finishing the Boston Marathon when his legs gave out. But as he struggled to get up, two other runners came along, lifted him up and helped him across the finish line.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived by popemobile before leading Mass on Sunday in the Angolan city of Kilamba.

video:video.video:description: Cuba's U.N. ambassador denied reports that Havana would release political prisoners as part of the renewed dialogue with the U.S. while asserting that officials are “preparing for all scenarios” if President Donald Trump makes good on his threats to intervene in the country.

video:video.video:description: A federal judge on Wednesday said rapper Pooh Shiesty will remain in custody until trial after he and eight others were accused of kidnapping three music industry professionals and robbing them at gunpoint in Texas.

video:video.video:title: Sumatran orangutan uses canopy bridge to cross road in Indonesia
video:video.video:description: A Sumatran orangutan has been filmed for the first time using a human-made canopy bridge to cross a public road on the Indonesian island of Sumatra, conservationists said Monday.

video:video.video:title: Patriarch Theophilos III washes feet of Orthodox priests in Jerusalem at ceremony ahead of Easter
video:video.video:description: Patriarch Theophilos III washed the feet of Orthodox priests in Jerusalem on Thursday in a ceremony made more special by the reopening of the Holy Sepulchre.

video:video.video:title: Former Australian soldier charged with committing five war crime murders in Afghanistan
video:video.video:description: Australia’s most decorated living veteran, Ben Roberts-Smith, faces war crime charges on allegations that he killed five unarmed Afghans while serving in Afghanistan from 2009 and 2012, police and media reported on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: A sheep gives birth to rare sextuplets on a Vermont farm
video:video.video:description: A sheep at Clover & Bee Farm in Underhill gave birth to a rare batch of six lambs earlier this month. (AP video: Amanda Swinhart)

video:video.video:title: How luxury watchmaking has become a fabric of Switzerland's heritage
video:video.video:description: Switzerland’s reputation for watchmaking has been built over centuries, with Geneva at the center of a global luxury industry defined by precision engineering and specialist craftsmanship.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out at Rio de Janeiro Olympic Park velodrome
video:video.video:description: Dozens of firefighters were battling a large blaze at Rio De Janeiro's Olympic Park Velodrome on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo calls for peace at start of his Africa tour
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called for peace and the end of “neocolonial tendencies” in world affairs on Monday during the first papal visit to Algeria, all while facing an extraordinary broadside by President Donald Trump over his criticism of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry speaks on legacy of Ukraine war, hopes global community can 'speak truth to power'
video:video.video:description: Britain's Prince Harry said on Friday that the legacy of any war is "huge" and that it's important to bring an end to global conflicts "sooner rather than later" during his third visit in a year to Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Wildfires burn in Florida
video:video.video:description: Wildfires burning across the southeastern U.S. were intensifying today. Some of the biggest blazes are along Georgia’s coast and around Jacksonville, Florida, a state that is facing one of its worst fire seasons in decades.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts emerge from their moon capsule after returning to Earth
video:video.video:description: The first astronauts to visit the moon in more than a half-century are back on Earth. Artemis II's crew of four have emerged one-by-one from their lunar capsule Friday after a splashdown in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: Shipping ramifications of Iran war compound suffering for the sick in war-torn Sudan
video:video.video:description: In a small clinic in a remote village in rural Sudan, the shelves of the pharmacy were starting to empty out, with the clinic unsure about when the next shipment of medicines would arrive.

video:video.video:title: Exonerees face roadblocks in the journey from prison to employment
video:video.video:description: People who are wrongfully incarcerated then exonerated, sometimes after spending decades behind bars, face yet more challenges finding jobs and rebuilding their lives after their release.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese find shelter on covered football pitch in Sidon
video:video.video:description: A covered football stadium in the town of Haret Saida has been turned into an emergency shelter as Lebanon struggles with a growing displacement crisis.

video:video.video:description: Officials say a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children, including seven of his own, and shot two other people Sunday in an early morning attack of domestic violence carried out across two houses.

video:video.video:title: Sparse shelves amid liquor shortage in Mississippi
video:video.video:description: A backlog at Mississippi's alcohol distribution center is causing shortages throughout the state. (AP Video: Sophie Bates)
