video:video.video:title: Peruvians choosing a president from 35-candidate pool in Sunday's election
video:video.video:description: International observers arrived in Lima ahead of the country's presidential election on Sunday. Voting is mandatory for Peruvians from the ages of 18 to 70.

video:video.video:title: Streep, Hathaway dazzle at London 'Prada' premiere
video:video.video:description: The main cast of "The Devil Wears Prada" showed off their style at the film's London premiere. (April 22)

video:video.video:title: Bryan Cranston shares memories daughter, Taylor Dearden, growing up on “Malcolm in the Middle” set.
video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of the “Malcolm In The Middle” revival, Bryan Cranston shared memories of his daughter, Taylor Dearden of “The Pitt”, hanging out with the child cast of “Malcolm In The Middle.”

video:video.video:title: Iran says it won’t hand over enriched uranium to the US
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh said his country isn't for a new round of face-to-face talks with the US because the Americans “have not abandoned their maximalist position.”

video:video.video:title: Funeral held for man killed in Israeli drone attack on Gaza City
video:video.video:description: An Israeli drone killed a Palestinian man Monday in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said. The strike hit a bike in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood in Gaza City and also wounded a child, according to the Shifa hospital which received the casualties.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he posted an image of himself as a 'doctor' not Jesus and won’t apologize to Pope Leo
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump admits to posting an image of himself as a healer, seeming to make comparisons between himself and Jesus Christ.

video:video.video:title: Young Palestinian artists in Gaza exhibit their impressions of war as a fragile ceasefire holds
video:video.video:description: Palestinian artists participated in an Art Festival in Central Gaza's Bureij on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum takes a firmer stance with Trump on migrant deaths and Cuba
video:video.video:description: Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum is taking a firmer stance against U.S. President Donald Trump, condemning migrant deaths in ICE detention and opposing policies toward Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Adrien Brody on Kim Kardashian joining producing team of Broadway's 'The Fear of 13'
video:video.video:description: Adrien Brody walked the red carpet opening night of Broadway's "The Fear of 13." The Oscar-winning actor says the play which had a prior run in London "resonates more" in New York.

video:video.video:title: Trump downplays Iran negotiations, says deal ‘makes no difference’
video:video.video:description: Speaking to reporters outside the White House on Saturday, U.S. President Donald Trump claimed military victory against Iran and downplayed the importance of ongoing ceasefire negotiations involving Vice President JD Vance because “regardless what happens we win.”

video:video.video:title: Trump-endorsed Republican Clay Fuller wins Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former House seat in Georgia
video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller has won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia. Fuller’s special election win Tuesday bolsters a slim GOP majority.

video:video.video:description: Authorities filed murder charges against the roommate of a Bangladeshi doctoral student who disappeared with his girlfriend from the University of South Florida.

video:video.video:title: Building roads can threaten the Amazon. But they may become dig sites that peer into the past
video:video.video:description: Paving roads in the Amazon rainforest often causes deforestation that threatens the people who live there.

video:video.video:title: New rare earth mineral facility using new extraction technique set to open in South Africa
video:video.video:description: A new rare earth mineral facility that uses what it claims to be a ground-breaking extraction technique is set to open in South Africa, with US investment.

video:video.video:title: French teen charged in Singapore over vending machine straw-licking video
video:video.video:description: A French teen faces charges in Singapore after posting a video of himself licking a straw from an orange juice vending machine and putting it back.

video:video.video:description: There are more tornadoes in the US than anywhere else on Earth. How do they form? This is in part because of the geography of the US.

video:video.video:description: Body camera footage shows the moment a New York City home exploded as police officers responded to a domestic dispute call.

video:video.video:description: Russian State Duma Speaker Vyacheslav Volodin said on Sunday that it was a “great honor” to take part in an inauguration ceremony of a military memorial in Pyongyang.

video:video.video:description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer described the two-week ceasefire as "highly fragile" and stressed that Lebanon should be included in it.

video:video.video:description: Offshore wind turbines roughly three times the height of the Statue of Liberty were spinning off the coast of Rhode Island Thursday, sending clean electricity to the region.

video:video.video:title: Philadelphia Museum of Art embraces Rocky Balboa statue's worldwide appeal
video:video.video:description: A new exhibition, “Rising Up: Rocky and the Making of Monuments,” brings the bronze figure of Rocky Balboa inside for the first time, reframing it as both pop culture icon and part of a 2,000-year tradition of boxing imagery.

video:video.video:title: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving in latest departure of a top defense leader
video:video.video:description: Navy Secretary John Phelan is leaving his job, the Pentagon abruptly announced Wednesday, the first head of a military service to depart during President Donald Trump’s second term but just the latest top defense leader to step down or be ousted.

video:video.video:description: "Charlie's Angels" Kate Jackson, Jaclyn Smith and Cheryl Ladd reunite to celebrate the show’s 50th anniversary at PaleyFest LA. (April 7)

video:video.video:description: A former student opened fire at a high school in southeast Turkey on Tuesday, wounding at least 16 people before killing himself, an official said.

video:video.video:title: Chinamaxxing and ‘becoming Chinese’ are trending and the state wants in
video:video.video:description: Young Western social media users are embracing a “very Chinese time” of their lives, and China’s government is keen to use this moment to boost the country’s cultural influence worldwide.

video:video.video:title: Archbishop of Miami speaks on Catholic charity funding cuts to house migrant children
video:video.video:description: Archbishop of Miami Thomas Wenski raises concerns about the Trump administration's decision to cancel an $11 million contract used to shelter migrant children entering the United States.

video:video.video:description: Six leading candidates for California governor are jostling for advantage in a chaotic race with no clear leader.

video:video.video:title: South Korea and New Zealand conduct joint amphibious drill
video:video.video:description: Troops from South Korea and New Zealand took part in an amphibious exercise off the coats of Pohang on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists Iranians want the US to 'keep bombing'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the Iranian people want the U.S. to "keep bombing," insisting citizens are “willing to suffer" in order to secure their freedom.

video:video.video:description: Both President Donald Trump and his wife, Melania, are calling on ABC to fire Jimmy Kimmel after the late-night comic joked last week that the first lady had “the glow of an expectant widow.”

video:video.video:description: As Earth warms due to human-caused climate change, the ground isn’t staying frozen as consistently in many places, causing more frequent runoff events and in turn, more winter days when nitrates are likely to reach unhealthy levels.

video:video.video:description: The head of the U.N. nuclear agency says the majority of Iran’s highly enriched uranium is still believed to be at its Isfahan nuclear complex.

video:video.video:description: The 28-foot statue of the famous RCA dog listening to a phonograph has become a local icon in Albany.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said he had “no fear of the Trump administration" when he responded to a reporter's question on board the papal plane en route to Algiers on Monday.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is embarking on the first-ever papal trip to Algeria on Monday, aiming to promote Christian-Muslim coexistence at a time of global conflict and honor the Algerian-born inspiration of his religious spirituality, St.

video:video.video:description: Oscar Isaac's kids want him to reunite with his old ska band. He and co-star Carey Mulligan shared their dream throwback music festival lineups while promoting the second season of "Beef."

video:video.video:description: People gathered outside of the Wisconsin governor's office demanding that the governor and attorney general try to shut down a beagle breeding and research facility where protesters clashed with police.

video:video.video:description: Newport, Rhode Island, has neighborhoods of opulent mansions with lots of mature trees along with neighborhoods of subsidized housing and far fewer trees.

video:video.video:description: Hydrologists working high in the Rocky Mountains have measured what they say was Colorado’s worst snowpack on record—an alarming fact for a state that supplies drinking water to millions across the drought-stricken American West.

video:video.video:description: A helicopter flying between palm oil plantations on Indonesia's Borneo Island has crashed, killing the eight people on board.

video:video.video:description: The Iranian Red Crescent on Friday posted a video on their social media account of a destroyed aid warehouse belonging to the organization in the Bushehr province, after it was hit by an American-Israeli airstrike.

video:video.video:description: On day 6 of their mission to the moon, the Artemis II astronauts woke up to the voice of Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell, who recorded the message just two months before his death last August.

video:video.video:title: Science to ancient octopus fossil: you’re not an octopus after all
video:video.video:description: Newly published research by the University of Reading concludes that fossilized remains listed by Guinness World Records as the earliest known octopus belong instead to a relative of a nautilus, a cephalopod with both tentacles and a shell.

video:video.video:title: Are we alone? Why the question of alien life keeps returning

video:video.video:description: Photographers from The Associated Press in Lebanon talked about the impactful images they took on Wednesday, the deadliest day in the country since the renewed war between Israel and Iranian-backed militant group, Hezbollah that erupted on March.

video:video.video:description: A group of former cleanup workers who returned to Chernobyl ahead of the 40th anniversary of the 1986 nuclear disaster, revisited a nearby site where they once worked to help contain the world’s worst nuclear accident.

video:video.video:title: Researchers create new potato varieties to strengthen nation’s chip supply

video:video.video:description: A new AP-NORC poll finds Hispanic adults who helped re-elect President Donald Trump in 2024 are now deeply unhappy with his performance. (AP Video: Nathan Ellgren)

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV walked Tuesday in the footsteps of his spiritual father, St. Augustine, making a pilgrimage to the archaeological ruins in Algeria where the fifth-century titan of early Christianity lived, died and wrote some of the most important works in Western thought.

video:video.video:description: A man accused of plotting to attack one of Taylor Swift’s concerts in Vienna nearly two years ago has pleaded guilty as his trial began on Tuesday.

video:video.video:description: Police say one person was shot and killed and six others were injured in what they call a mass shooting at a Chick-fil-A in Union, New Jersey.

video:video.video:description: A new reality is setting in for air travelers worldwide: rising fees, fewer flight options and difficult decisions about whether a trip is worth the cost.

video:video.video:description: Moves toward internet control for years, Russia has sought to take the internet under total government control and potentially cut it off from the rest of the world.

video:video.video:description: After declaring the Strait of Hormuz open, Tehran maintained its grip on transit and threatened to close it again if the US kept in place its blockade of Iranian ships and ports.

video:video.video:description: A group of critically endangered mountain bongos bred in European zoos are being prepared for transfer back to Kenya, as part of a long term effort to rebuild wild populations of one of Africa’s rarest large mammals.

video:video.video:description: Oil prices are plunging back toward $95 per barrel, and stock markets are surging worldwide after President Donald Trump pulled back from his threat to force a whole civilization to die in the war with Iran.

video:video.video:description: The historic midnight ride of Paul Revere was reenacted Monday but with some modern-day tweaks: It was run in the middle of the day, and the horse and rider had a police escort.

video:video.video:description: In Peacock's "The Miniature Wife," Elizabeth Banks plays a woman shrunk by her husband, played by Matthew Macfadyen. (April 17)

video:video.video:description: A likely tornado tore through a small village in northwest Illinois on Friday, ripping down power lines and roofs, uprooting trees.

video:video.video:title: Oregon Zoo welcomes two penguin chicks

video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip continue to face uncertainty and fading hope six months after a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold.

The Artemis II mission will not land on the lunar surface, but it's a critical step in testing deep space travel and the crew reflects a new generation of international and more diverse human spaceflight.

video:video.video:description: King Charles III is only the second British monarch to address a joint session of Congress, after his mother, Queen Elizabeth II, delivered a similar speech in 1991.

video:video.video:description: The toilet on Artemis II can be used as normal, entry flight director Rick Henfling said. It malfunctioned following Wednesday's liftoff and has been hit-or-miss ever since.

video:video.video:description: In Mexico, a baby monkey is growing up clinging to a plush toy after being rejected by his mother, offering a glimpse into the delicate process of assisted animal care.

video:video.video:description: The long-awaited trade deal between South American bloc Mercosur and the European Union takes effect Friday, at least provisionally.

Japan Airlines and a robotic company announced that they will test humanoid robots next month to potentially help with handling baggage, cleaning cabins, and operating ground support equipmen

video:video.video:description: A gunman who killed at least six people in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital, before taking hostages and barricading himself inside a supermarket on Saturday has been shot and killed by police, Ukrainian officials said.

video:video.video:description: Trump’s approval rating on the economy dropped to 30% in April from 38% in a March AP-NORC poll. (AP Video by Nathan Ellgren)

video:video.video:description: Russia and China on Tuesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz that had been repeatedly watered down in hopes those two countries would abstain.

video:video.video:title: New dad Justin Theroux 'beyond proud' of baby boy
video:video.video:description: Justin Theroux and his wife, Nicole Brydon Bloom, who recently welcomed a baby boy, enjoyed a night out at the premiere of his new film, "The Devil Wears Prada 2."

video:video.video:title: Video shows suspect running toward correspondents' dinner
video:video.video:description: Security video appears to show suspect running towards White House correspondents' dinner

video:video.video:title: Palestinians mourn Al Jazeera journalist Mohammad Wishah targeted by Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Palestinians gathered Thursday morning at Al-Aqsa hospital in central Gaza to bid a final farewell to Al Jazeera correspondent Mohammed Wishah, who was killed in an Israeli drone strike the day before.

video:video.video:title: US energy blockade leaves Cuban farmers struggling to feed a nation
video:video.video:description: In Cuba farmers are struggling to grow and transport food. (AP/ Danica Coto, Ariel Fernández and Milexsy Durán)

video:video.video:title: Nutella jar seen spinning through Artemis II
video:video.video:description: Minutes before the Artemis II mission shattered the distance record set by Apollo 13 in 1970 a jar of Nutella hazelnut spread was seen spinning through the spacecraft.

video:video.video:title: Michigan fans gather outside basketball arena in Ann Arbor to welcome the 2026 national champs home
video:video.video:description: Michigan fans gathered outside Crisler Center to welcome the freshly crowned 2026 men’s basketball national champions back to Ann Arbor (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Paris Fury's parenting advice for Molly Mae and Tommy Fury
video:video.video:description: Boxer Tyson Fury's wife Paris passes on family advice to brother-in-law Tommy Fury and his partner, reality TV star Molly-Mae Hague.

video:video.video:title: Funeral of 13 Lebanese service officers killed in Israeli strike on southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Thirteen members of Lebanon’s State Security Service were laid to rest on Saturday in Sidon, a day after being killed in an Israeli strike in the southern city of Nabatieh.

video:video.video:title: Fans react to opening night of BTS' world tour
video:video.video:description: Fans react to BTS’ return to the stage after nearly four years, at the opening night of the K-pop megastars' "ARIRANG" world tour.

video:video.video:title: Iran to take 'serious legal steps' to protect its cultural sites
video:video.video:description: Iranian Minister of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts Sayed Reza Salihi-Amiri said on Sunday that Iran would take "serious legal steps" to protect its cultural sites, after Israeli and U.S. bombardments have damaged over 100 sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: How are rising prices affecting people’s choices?
video:video.video:description: Rising prices are still hitting consumers, but PepsiCo says lowering prices helped bring shoppers back. Here’s how people are feeling about higher costs.

video:video.video:title: 'Ketamine Queen' who supplied drugs to Matthew Perry set for sentencing
video:video.video:description: A woman who sold actor Matthew Perry the drug ketamine that killed him is set to be sentenced Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Eight children between ages three and 12 died after a mass shooting in Louisiana, police say
video:video.video:description: A man killed eight children, including seven of his own, and shot two women in an attack in a Shreveport, Louisiana, neighborhood that was the nation’s deadliest shooting in more than two years, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Tension remain high at Israel-Lebanon border as Hezbollah attacks continue to set off sirens
video:video.video:description: Tensions remained high on the Israeli-Lebanese border as rocket and drone attacks by the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah continued to set off sirens throughout the day on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Baby camel born in UK zoo
video:video.video:description: Sophia was born to Bactrian camels mum Izzy and dad Oakley at Whipsnade Zoo in Bedfordshire, boosting the herd’s total to eight.

video:video.video:title: Find out what King Charles III will do on his state visit to America
video:video.video:description: King Charles III will make his first official state visit as monarch to the United States in late April, visiting Washington, D.C., New York and Virginia.

video:video.video:title: Measles threatens babies in South Carolina and the nation as community vaccination rates decline
video:video.video:description: Babies too young to be vaccinated are among the most vulnerable in measles outbreaks like the one in South Carolina, which is the largest the U.S. has seen in decades.

video:video.video:title: China is rapidly embracing AI across many tech sectors
video:video.video:description: From police patrol robots to everyday consumer gadgets, artificial intelligence is taking centre stage at this year’s Canton Fair in China.

video:video.video:title: Timeline of Minnesota fraud investigations
video:video.video:description: Minnesota has been beset by a series of complex, overlapping fraud investigations that have stretched over several years and involve potentially billions of dollars in wasted federal funding.

video:video.video:title: 1 killed, 2 presumed dead after partial roof collapse at parking garage being built in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: Authorities say a stairwell roof at a parking garage being built in Philadelphia collapsed suddenly, killing one person and leaving two missing and presumed dead.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Eastern Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy honored with prestigious International Four Freedoms Award for Ukraine's resilience
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was awarded the International Four Freedoms Award at a ceremony Thursday for his and his nation's courage and resilience in resisting the full-scale invasion launched more than four years ago by Russia.

video:video.video:title: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive in US for four-day state visit
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla arrived in the U.S. Monday for a four-day state visit marking America’s 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Haitian community leader in Ohio hopes SCOTUS does not remove protected status
video:video.video:description: Rose-Thamar Joseph listened to arguments at the US Supreme Court as justices weighed the Trump administration’s push to end legal protections for Haitians and Syrians as migrants fleeing war and natural disaster.

video:video.video:title: Blind Massachusetts man is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets
video:video.video:description: Matthew Shifrin is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets using free accessibility instructions created by sighted and blind volunteers in his nonprofit group.

video:video.video:title: Pressure mounts on Kanye West to be pulled from headline role at summer music festival in London
video:video.video:description: Pressure was mounting Sunday on the American rapper Kanye West to be pulled from his headline role at a London music festival this summer.

video:video.video:title: Price hikes hit Greeks shopping in preparation for Orthodox Easter celebration
video:video.video:description: Price increases due to the US-Israel war on Iran have hit Greek shoppers as they prepare to celebrate Orthodox Easter on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Khartoum remains quiet three years into the war in Sudan
video:video.video:description: Three years into the war and a year after Khartoum was taken over by the Sudanese army, the battered heart of the capital city remains quiet.

video:video.video:title: Eyewitnesses recount three deadly Israeli strikes on medics in southern Lebanon
video:video.video:description: “We went to help. These aren't just civilians, they're our colleagues, so it was a little emotional for us,” Mohammed Jaber, 43, told The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Ex-Michigan coach Sherrone Moore gets probation for confrontation with woman who disclosed affair
video:video.video:description: Former Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore was placed on probation over a confrontation with his executive assistant soon after he was fired for having an inappropriate relationship with her (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: ‘Dances With Wolves’ actor Nathan Chasing Horse sentenced to life in prison for sexual assault
video:video.video:description: Nathan Chasing Horse has been sentenced to life in prison for sexual assault. The “Dances With Wolves” actor was found guilty in January of 13 charges in a yearslong sexual abuse case.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV celebrates outdoor Mass in Saurimo, in northeastern Angola
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV celebrated Mass in the northeastern Angolan city of Saurimo on Monday, as his Africa trip continues.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II’s astronauts return to Earth with a splashdown in the Pacific
video:video.video:description: The three Americans and one Canadian have returned with a dramatic splashdown Friday evening, as their capsule parachuted into the Pacific to close out a nearly 10-day trip to the moon and back.

video:video.video:title: Federal Reserve keeps key rate unchanged; four officials dissent, most in almost 34 years
video:video.video:description: The Federal Reserve left its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 3.6% for the third straight meeting.

video:video.video:title: 'Malcolm in the Middle' cast returns for more chaos
video:video.video:description: Bryan Cranston, Frankie Muniz, and Jane Kaczmarek reunited Tuesday in New York to celebrate the new Hulu limited series “Malcolm in the Middle: Life’s Still Unfair."

video:video.video:title: Explosion and fire collapses home in New York amid domestic dispute
video:video.video:description: New York police search for a suspect after a house collapsed in a massive explosion and fire in Queens; appears to have started as a domestic dispute

video:video.video:title: Heavy rain and snowmelt is pushing large chunks of ice into northeastern Michigan homes
video:video.video:description: Homes have flooded along Black Lake in Michigan's northeastern Lower Peninsula as spring rains and winter melt overflow stressed dam systems

video:video.video:title: Jon Hamm compares working on 'The Morning Show' with 'Your Friends and Neighbors'
video:video.video:description: Jon Hamm compares the “murderer’s row” of talent on "The Morning Show" to the Broadway-caliber lineup on "Your Friends and Neighbors." (April 15)
